entry.title: Exporting comments in SAC stories based on live models
entry.content:
Technical Pre-requisites
SAP Analytics Cloud:
. QRC2.2023 for a QRC based tenant. To use the formatting option you will need QRC3.2023.Wave 4 2023 for fast track tenants.
Supported SAP BW versions for adding comments on tables:
. SAP BW/4HANA 2.0 SP13 or higher. From SP07 to SP12, SAP Note 3244228 Information published on SAP site is required.
. SAP BW/4HANA 2021 SP04 or higher. From SP00 to SP03, SAP Note 3244228 Information published on SAP site is required.
. SAP BW 7.50 SP23 or higher, and SAP Note 3242613 Information published on SAP site, 3133846 Information published on SAP site, and 3169011 Information published on SAP site are required.
. BW 7.57 on HANA, SP00

Unlike the comments at widget level Data point comments are available for each cell values in a table in a story and this is stored in the BW system .

To add comment in a story we need to right click on the data point and select add comment as shown in the screenshot . Initially this section will be greyed out as we have not enabled the comment for the table 

Enabling data point Commenting feature in Properties section 

    To enable comment select the Table go to the builder panel and select  properties section and select allow data point comment once this option is selected, we should be able to add comment to the data point

 

Adding Comments to a data cell 

 Once add data point comment is selected it will open pop up where we can enter our comment for a value which we/user is interested in .Normally this can be a situation where we have a variance which is not with in the limit and concerned person needs to put a comment for this variance .

 

Once the comment got added to the data cell the data cell will get highlighted with a symbol and color as shown 

 

Seeing the added Comment

By hovering on the symbol we can see the comment which we added and the timestamp

 

Adding new comment 

To add a new comment we need to click on the edit comment option and Replace the existing comment with new comment which will create a new comment and will also retain the previous comment .

 

Seeing Previous Comment and history 

To see the previous comment and history we need to select the clock symbol on clicking clock symbol it will open up the pop up which will show the latest comment and the previous comments

 

Deleting the Comment

Clicking on the delete button will delete the latest comment and historical comments as well, I was unable to

 

find option to delete individual comments .

Exporting the comments using export to pdf option 

If we export the comments by default it won't show the comments in the exported file as the comment is not visible in the table unless we hover over the comment symbol .To rectify this issue we need to add a comment column next to the column which contains the data cell. this can be done by right clicking the column name and add client calculation and select comment 

After doing the above step new comment column will get added which will show the added comments 

Exporting the story after adding the comment column will export the visible comments as well.

Limitations which we observed 

1) No option to delete individual comments 

2)Only latest comments can be exported using export to pdf .

entry.title: Establishing the Connection From SAC to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am Abdul Suhana, a Technology-Associate Analytics Consultant working in at Phoenix Business Consulting. I would like to share how to establish the Connection From SAC to SAP Datasphere

Overview: This blog post will explain the step by step process of Establishing the Connection From SAC to SAP Datasphere. Whether you're new to this configuration, this information will guide you to establish the connection quickly.

Prerequisites:

  • SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

Please follow the below steps.,

Step1: Log in to SAP Datasphere Account, Go to System -- Administration--App Integration

Here in the Trusted Origin-> Add the SAP Analytics Cloud URL and click on Save

Step2: Login to SAP Analytics Cloud, Go to Connection Tab

Click on New connection.

Select the data source. Click on dropdown ->Connect to Live Data Data source

Select -> SAP Datasphere

Step3: Enter the Name and Description of the connection in the Sap Datasphere Connection Details, give the URL of the SAP Datasphere system under the ‘Host’.

           Enter Port number-> 443

Click on Ok, then enter the Username and Password of the SAP Datasphere system.


Output: Connection established

Step4: Now Login into SAC Account, Go to stories and Select the Optimized Design Experience.

Select the chart and choose Database as DWC

Click on DATASPHERE_SPACE folder and select the model.

Here in this space we can see the analytical models if created.

Step5: Choose the Analytical Model in stories

Double click on the model, and we can able to access the stories in SAC.

Voila!!! Now we can create Stories in our SAC account with datasphere connection.

If you find my blog useful, please like and share your feedback in the comments below.

Thanks,

Abdul Suhana

entry.title: SAP 分析云 2024.13版功能更新
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能（BI）、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。 

本文介绍了 SAP 分析云 2024.13 版本的新功能。这些新功能已经在 SAP 分析云 FastTrack 客户的系统上线。对于 SAP 分析云季度发布周期（QRC）客户，此版本及其功能将作为 QRC 2024年第3季度版本的一部分提供。在此处可以查看 QRC 发布时间表。   

 

系统和技术要求 

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本1.0.409，以利用所有数据采集类型。  

代理的最低版本仍为 1.0.75，但以下情况除外： 

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库的版本 1.0.99 
  • SAP universe （UNX） 的版本 1.0.365（SAP 分析云代理需要其他设置步骤，有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP 说明 3262098） 
  • SAP Business Warehouse （BW） 的版本 1.0.353 
  • SAP HANA View 的版本 1.0.235 
  • File Server版本 1.0.248 

有关详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。 

有关SAP BW与SAP分析云集成的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅SAP Note 2541557了解更多详细信息。 

有关通过 SAP分析云的 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 实时访问Universe时的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921了解更多详细信息。 

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的简易部署工具包。 

  • 注意：为避免下载旧版本，以及可能让用户面对Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的不当绑定问题，只有三个最新版本的工具包可供下载。 

有关云代理版本变更管理详情，请参阅 SAP Note 3264839。 

  

更新亮点 

  • 管理和服务管理 
    • 数据审计：能够根据其他过滤器删除审计数据 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
    • 禁用对新用户的欢迎邮件 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
  • 面向计划和分析的人工智能 
    • Just Ask 比较 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
  • 数据探索 
    • 数据分析器- 在工具内分享见解 
    • 数据分析器 - 快捷链接 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
    • 计算对话框：LOG、LOG10、CEIL、ROUND、TRUNC 
    • 生成器面板/可用对象列表中的上下文菜单增强功能 
    • 更多筛选成员功能（带交换/删除功能的筛选；元组筛选的上下文突出显示）|（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
  • 企业计划 
    • 数据操作 - 映射策略“属性” |（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
    • 多步操作 – 由 BI 用户创建和更新 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
  • Microsoft Office 集成 
    • 引用成员的前端计算 
    • 消空 
  • 故事设计 
    • 优化故事体验中的图表智能分组 
    • BW 层次结构的公共 URL API 支持 
  • 故事可拓展性 
    • 支持图例的自定义微件插件 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
    • 在自定义微件中创建计算维度或基于帐户的维度，如图表和表格 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)  

 

管理和服务管理

数据审计：能够根据其他过滤器删除审计数据

在删除数据更改以降低数据更改日志的大小时，现在可以根据模型的维度以及审核时间、审核用户和审核操作添加过滤器，以控制哪些数据更改需要删除。（SAP 路线图浏览器) 

 

禁用对新用户的欢迎邮件 

现在您可以禁止向添加到租户的新用户发送欢迎电子邮件。有关详情，请参阅管理欢迎电子邮件通知。（SAP 路线图浏览器) 

 

 面向计划和分析的人工智能 

JUST ASK 比较 

现在，您可以在“JUST ASK”中使用以下内容：（SAP 路线图浏览器) 

  • 比较两个度量值：比较销售额和支出 
  • 比较两个度量值和一个维度：按地点比较销售额和支出 
  • 时间比较：比较当年各地点的销售额和支出 
  • 结合比较和计算：比较各地的平均销售额和各地本年度的平均支出  
  • 将版本维度与度量值比较相结合：比较加拿大的实际销售额和预测销售额 

 

 请注意, 目前Just Ask功能在阿里云平台上的SAP 分析云中暂时不可用。 

 

数据探索 

数据分析器 - 分享见解 

现在，您可以使用新的“共享”工具栏按钮中的“共享见解”功能，直接从数据分析器共享见解。在“共享见解”对话框中，可以添加要与之共享见解的用户或团队，设置访问类型，以及设置新收件人是否会收到电子邮件。 

数据分析器 - 快捷方式链接 

如果您想在数据分析器中捕捉当前数据视图的状态而无需保存，现在可以使用工具栏中新的 "共享 "按钮中的 "创建快捷方式链接 "功能来创建快捷链接。 生成的快捷链接可以复制到剪贴板，从而添加到电子邮件或在新标签页中打开。 系统配置区域会安排一项清理任务，以定期删除快捷链接。 作为管理员，您可以重新配置默认周期。（SAP 路线图浏览器) 

 

 计算对话框：LOG、LOG10、CEIL、ROUND、TRUNC 

我们引入了对两个数学函数和三个转换函数的支持： 

  • LOG（number） 和 LOG10（number） 分别计算正数的自然对数和以 10 为底的对数对数 
  • CEIL 返回一个大于或等于输入数字的实数 
  • ROUND 四舍五入到指定的小数位数 
  • TRUNC 在指定的小数位截断提供的数字 

这些新功能增强了BW和HANA系统中的数据操作，使数据排列更精确和先进。 

功能分类： 

  • 函数现在分为以下类别：转换、逻辑、日期和时间、数学、查找和参考、业务和动态时间。 
  • 在函数部分添加了一个新的搜索框，以便更快地访问函数，并在搜索过程中保持类别清晰。 

具有搜索功能的新分类布局增强了用户体验并简化了计算生成过程。 

生成器面板/可用对象列表中的上下文菜单增强功能

在数据分析器中，添加了多项增强功能以改进用户工作流程：

  • 添加了拆分器和“展开/折叠”按钮，确保更好地管理数据分析器“生成器”面板中的空间。
  • 在“可用对象”列表中，现在可以使用带有过滤功能的 "更多 "菜单。
  • 在“可用对象”列表中，现在可以通过鼠标右键单击功能使用上下文菜单。
  • 在“可用对象”列表中，现在列出了帐户/度量的所有成员，而不仅仅是已预订的成员

 

更多筛选成员功能（带交换/删除功能的筛选；元组筛选的上下文突出显示）

表格上下文菜单增加了额外的筛选器选项：（SAP 路线图浏览器)

筛选成员并

  • 交换维的对象：此上下文菜单可让用户将过滤成员应用于该选区，并与要放置在轴上的另一个维交换。
  • 删除维：此上下文菜单可让用户将过滤成员应用于该选区，并从坐标轴上删除维度。
  • 筛选颜色突出显示：具有不同筛选选项（筛选成员、筛选、排除成员、排除、筛选多个成员）的颜色突出显示。

 

 

企业计划

数据操作 - 映射策略“属性”

在跨模型复制步骤中，现在可以选择属性用于同名映射。借助此功能，用户可以通过在建模器中维护附加属性来完成自动映射维度

 

多步操作 – 由 BI 用户创建和更新

标准“BI 管理员”角色现在包含多步操作权限。拥有 BI 许可证的用户能够创建和执行仅包含 BI 步骤的多步操作（不包括 BI 嵌入式许可证）。此外，操作触发器的名称（如多步操作触发器、数据操作触发器和 BPC 计划序列触发器）已名为启动器（多步操作启动器、数据操作启动器和 BPC 计划序列启动器）。

 

 

Microsoft Office 集成

引用成员的前端计算

在前端计算中，现在可以引用维度成员和属性。

这样，就可以将SAP分析云和 Excel 功能结合起来，因为您可以在公式中使用 XLOOKUP、IF 和更多 Excel 功能。

此功能自 2024 年 6 月 19 日起可供客户使用。

 

消空

现在，您可以使用“消空”选项来消除表中的空值 （-）。该选项在“行”或“列”的上下文菜单中可用。

使用现有的“消零”选项，可以在一个步骤中消除零 （0） 和空值 （-）。

此功能自 2024 年 6 月 19 日起可供客户使用。

 

故事设计

优化故事体验中的图表智能分组

现在，您可以在优化故事体验的气泡图中将智能分组应用于图表。

 

BW 层次结构的公共 URL API 支持

借助 SAP分析云URL API，可以筛选时间层次结构和父子层次结构，而BW Live Data 连接并不默认使用这些层次结构。F<XX>Hierarchy 参数支持对具有不同层次结构值的时间和父子层次结构进行筛选。

这一功能允许开发人员为内嵌层次结构节点过滤器的故事生成 URL，从而增强最终用户体验。 用户将受益于经过预过滤的故事，这些故事将呈现特定层次节点的特定数据。

参数语法：f<XX>Hierarchy=<HierarchyName>

  • <XX> 是 01 到 99 之间的任意数字。如果有多个用于筛选的筛选器，则为每个筛选器分配一个唯一的 <XX> 值。 <HierarchyName> 是要筛选的层次结构的名称。

这仅适用于优化故事体验。

故事可拓展性

支持图例的自定义微件插件

用户现在可以开发自己的微件插件来自定义图表图例。这项新功能增强了数据展示的整体故事体验。支持的图表类型有条形图/柱形图、堆积条形图/柱形图、堆积面积图和折线图。（SAP 路线图浏览器)

有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP分析云微件插件开发人员指南。

 

在自定义微件的图和表中创建计算维度或基于帐户的维度

在优化故事体验中，您可以在自定义微件中创建和使用计算维度或基于账户的维度。（SAP 路线图浏览器)

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和改进功能的高级概述。您可以在针对FastTrack客户的应用内帮助指南中找到有关这些功能的更多详细信息，如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期发布的信息，请参阅这篇SAP 知识库文章。

如果您对本博文的内容有任何疑问，可以在下方发表评论。如果您有具体的产品或技术问题，请在我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 问答论坛上提问。访问我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 社区页面，了解更多产品信息、卓越实践等。查看我们的 SAP 路线图浏览器，了解 SAP Analytics Cloud 即将推出的更多功能。

entry.title: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q3 2024
entry.content:
With Q3 2024 fast approaching we bring you some exciting and eagerly anticipated new features in SAP Analytics Cloud. We will first take a sneak peek in to our Top 5 features in QRC3 2024. A full list of what is coming can be found on our Roadmap Explorer.

Live SAP BW – Report-Report Interface (RRI)

A much-anticipated new feature, particularly for our BW customers is the Report-Report Interface (RRI).  This allows end users to use the RRI to jump from the executed query (sender) to another report (receiver) as maintained in the RRI back-end transaction (RSBBS):

Supported report types include:​

  • BW Query​
  • Transaction ​
  • Web Address​
  • ABAP Report​

Opens the receiver report of type:

  • BW Query in the Data Analyzer



Just Ask Enhancements

Just Ask was launched for the first time at the beginning of 2024, we are delighted to show you all the new features we have added for Natural Language Queries:

First, we add the ability to Hide model objects. This enables administrators to simplify models that are available for questions. We also have a number of enhancements to natural language understanding:

Just Ask: Prompt Guide

A Prompt Guide is now available to provide users with guidance for best usage of Just Ask features. The guide includes:

  • Examples questions to query
  • Highlights of key features
  • Tips to optimize performance 

Just Ask: Display Dimension Attributes

Dimension attributes for models in Just Ask are now visible in the following:

  • When selecting the + button next to next to the search bar
  • Model Overview tab (in the Manage Model area)
  • Explore Data tab (in the Manage Model area)
  • In the Rules section

Just Ask Comparison

You can now use the following in Just Ask questions: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • Compare two measures: Compare sales with expenses
  • Compare two measures and a dimension: Compare sales with expenses by location
  • Time comparisons: Compare sales with expenses by location in current year
  • Combine a comparison and a calculation: Compare average sales by location with average expenses by location this year
  • Combine a version dimension with a measure comparison: Compare actual sales with forecast in Canada

Plan Entry – data file upload (End-user)

An exciting new feature for our Planning users is the introduction of a native story trigger and process to allow mass upload of data by end-users. 

  • Data Management space in the modeler will include a new “Upload Jobs” section
  • Modeler will create an upload job by wrangling a template file which reflects the basic structure of the file the end-user is expected to load for that job
  • Data import trigger/widget can be inserted in the story by the story author and reference a previously defined model upload job

Standard Unit Conversion

Another exciting new Planning feature allows Modelers and Planners create measures and conversion measures of type Unit, to perform standard unit conversion, such length, weight, height. 

A unit conversion measure will convert source values from the base measure to the specified target units, that belong to the same unit group, using values from the standard unit conversion table. Some examples of unit groups include Length, Density and Weight.

You can also add calculated measures based on the unit conversion, or work with the converted values in your stories and for planning purposes.

The Standard Unit Conversion table is available for reference from the Conversions tab of the Modeller.  

 

More details can be found in this great blog on Planning extensions by @AmandaJMurphy 

Integration with Microsoft PowerPoint

Last but by no means least we are delighted to announce the SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint Microsoft PowerPoint. You can now intuitively add accurate and trusted data to your PowerPoint presentations, reusing your existing SAP Analytics Cloud widgets.

SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint will enable users to:

  • Intuitively connect to an SAP Analytics Cloud tenant
  • Readily find SAP Analytics Cloud stories and associated widgets
  • Select a widget and filter its data, if necessary
  • Add the widget to the slides
  • Refresh the widget's data on demand

 

There are plenty more exciting deliverables coming your way this quarter and we will be taking a deeper dive into these items in our What’s New video and blog series so stay tuned. For a full list of all features coming in QRC3 2024 for SAP Analytics Cloud check out the Roadmap Explorer

Want to experience new and existing features for yourself? Take the leap and start your journey towards making data-driven decisions with confidence by signing up for a free trial, today.

#sap #sapanalytics

entry.title: SAP Data and Analytics: Join our Performance focused Live Sessions Series
entry.content:
Performance optimization is a central task for every developer, data modeler, data engineer or data analyst since in addition to quality, speed is a key requirement for modern reporting. With this in mind, we have created a sophisticated series of expert-guided Live Sessions to address this important challenge.  

Boost your skills in SAP Analytics Cloud performance and explore key features and optimizations that enhance data analytics efficiency and effectiveness in SAP Analytics Cloud, in our specialized expert-guided Live Session events, “SAP Analytics Cloud Performance overview”. Register here.   

Understand the performance implications of story design and scripting in SAP Analytics Cloud, in our expert-guided Live Session events "Understanding the relationship between story design and performance in SAP Analytics Cloud". Register here. 

Discover best practices in data modeling in SAP HANA to ensure your models run at the highest possible speed and are built for sustainability, in our expert-guided Live Session events “Best practices when developing calculation views in SAP HANA”. Register here.   

Maximize the performance of accessing data in SAP BW/4HANA. Master the techniques for increasing efficiency and speeding up the retrieval of critical data in your organization, in our expert-guided Live Session events “Performance aspects of Advanced DataStore Objects and CompositeProviders in SAP BW/4HANA.” Register here.  

The Live Session events mentioned above are linked to the Learning Hub and can only be attended with a valid subscription.  

Product Learning CoE Analytics Team

entry.title: Leverage Data Export Service to View Currency Table Data
entry.content:
One requirement is to Export the content of SAC Currency Table and view it in a Story. Today, There is no option to export the content of Currency Table either as csv file or view the contents of the Currency Table in a Story or Dashboard.

In this blog, I will introduce you a way to achieve this by leveraging the Data Export Service and OData Import Connection without external tool or platform.

Prerequisite:

Your SAC tenant is a Cloud Foundry Tenant deployed either in AWS, Azure, Ali Cloud or Google Cloud.

Setup OAuth Client

Please follow the exact step 1, 2, 3 and 4 of Amanda’s blog on how to configure the OAuth Client in your SAC tenant.

Create an OData Connection

Create an OData connection on the source tenant (SAME tenant pointing to itself).

**Note that a new connection is required for each currency table as every connection is specific to a currency table on the source system.

Refer to the steps outlined in Amanda’s Blog for creation of a new Odata Connection, which will be used later to create a Model and setup the import Job for the Model to load the Currency Data from the Currency Table.

I will be outlining the steps below to generate the ‘Data Service URL’ which will be used during the creation of Odata connection.

Step 1: Use the endpoint to derive the ‘Data Service URL’ for the currency Table using the GET request type.

You will require the NamespaceID and ProviderID to call the endpoint.

NamespaceID: It is ‘sac_currency_tables’ for the Currency Table.  You can also find Namespace ID using the GET request on the below API endpoint.               

Sample:

https://your-sac-tenant-url.cloud/api/v1/dataexport/administration/Namespaces 

** use the above endpoint link and replace it with your SAC Tenant. 

ProviderID: It is the technical name of the currency table. You can also find ProviderID using the GET request on the below API endpoint. 

https://your-sac-tenant-url.cloud/api/v1/dataexport/administration/Namespaces(NamespaceID=' sac_currency_tables')/Providers 

** use the above endpoint link and replace it with your SAC Tenant.

Above request will list down all the Currency Table available for your tenant. As highlighted below, copy the ‘ServiceURL’ for the relevant currency table you want to view the data.

API Response

Step 2: Paste the Service URL in the ‘Data Service URL’.

Odata Connection Setup Screen

 

Step 3: Enter the remaining required parameters such as

  • Set Authentication Type to OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials.
  • The OAuth Client ID from Section A OAuth Client Setup
  • The Secret from Section A OAuth Client Setup
  • The Token URL from Section A OAuth Client Setup

Refer to Amanda Blog on the same for step step by details.

Setup the Model

Once the Connection is created, Next Step is to

  1. Setup a New Model to load the currency table data.
  2. Setup an Import Job based on the above connection to load the data.

Follow the steps provided in William Blog to achieve the above steps.

Sample Model

                                                                             

Sample Story

Summary

  1. This approach can be handy when the end user wants to see the currency rates-maintained in the table directly in a Story or a Dashboard without navigating to the currency table, which can also lead to user changing the rates by mistake.
